title: PlausiDen — blog
description: Notes on building a typed-substrate platform.

sections.title: Notes from the build.
sections.lede: Architectural decisions, post-mortems, and the occasional rant about static typing.

sections.body: Posts here trace a real decision — what we tried, what failed, what we kept. No vendor tips. No SEO-optimised listicles. The audit chain is the proof; the writing explains the why.

sections.text: Six most recent — chronological, not algorithmic. Subscribe via the form below or via RSS at /blog.rss.

sections.caption: Build notes — what we tried, what didn't work, what shipped.

sections.text: Each post traces a real architectural decision — what we tried, what didn't work, what shipped.

items.term: Loom catalogue: full primitive count shipped
items.definition: T660 P5+P6 closed. 101 new variants across layout, editorial, marketing, media, commerce, social, forms, nav, feedback, game/forum/video — plus the auth + Crucible widget batch.
forge content / forge search / forge assets subcommands wired. 21 tests added, three new CLI families.

items.term: Why LFI gates AI, not generates it
items.definition: The architectural inversion. Critic trait as compiler-enforced seam. Why bypass isn't a code-review policy — it's a type error.
Every named theme shares the same OKLCH palette. AMOLED variant has #000 bg + per-token contrast. Battery + readability.

items.term: Catching CVSS 4.0 in the supply chain
items.definition: Two transitive deps flagged at the cargo-deny step. CVSS 4.0 vector format support landed at the same time.

items.term: SLSA-attested SBOMs, the silent way they fail
items.definition: Our supply-chain CI was emitting zero SBOMs for weeks because the glob path mismatched cargo-cyclonedx's per-crate output. Fixed + hardened.

sections.heading: Get the build notes
sections.lede: One email a fortnight. No tracking pixels. We send from our own SMTP.

sections.title: Catch up with the build.
sections.lede: Every post traces a real architectural decision — what we tried, what didn't work, what shipped.
